documentation.readme: A tool for generating multiple types of NTLMv2 hash theft files.
usage_sections: Getting Started: These instructions will show you the requirements for and how to use ntlmtheft.

arguments.name: --filename
arguments.description: The base filename without extension, can be renamed later (eg: test, Board-Meeting2020, Bonus_Payment_Q4)

arguments.name: --generate
arguments.description: Choose to generate all files or a specific filetype

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: : The IP address of your SMB hash capture server (Responder, impacket ntlmrelayx, Metasploit auxiliary/server/capture/smb, etc)

arguments.name: --server
arguments.description: : The IP address of your SMB hash capture server (Responder, impacket ntlmrelayx, Metasploit auxiliary/server/capture/smb, etc)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with swisskyrepo/PayloadsAllTheThings (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with skylot/jadx (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, ad] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: A tool for generating multiple types of NTLMv2 hash theft files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A tool for generating multiple types of NTLMv2 hash theft files.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip3 install xlsxwriter. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Created: test/test.scf (BROWSE)`. Further invocations from upstream: `Created: test/test-(url).url (BROWSE)`; `Created: test/test-(icon).url (BROWSE)`. A tool for generating multiple types of NTLMv2 hash theft files.

name: ntlm_theft
summary: A tool for generating multiple types of NTLMv2 hash theft files.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need post_exploitation, use ntlm_theft because A tool for generating multiple types of NTLMv2 hash theft files.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Created: test/test.scf (BROWSE)` as documented upstream for ntlm_theft.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Created: test/test-(url).url (BROWSE)` as documented upstream for ntlm_theft.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Created: test/test-(icon).url (BROWSE)` as documented upstream for ntlm_theft.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Created: test/test.rtf (OPEN)` as documented upstream for ntlm_theft.
When chaining post_exploitation tools, pair ntlm_theft with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ntlm_theft --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
